# UC-10.12.55 · Anomalous Medical Record Number Access Patterns

## Description

Flags clinical or operational identities that open a statistically unusual number of distinct patient records within an hour compared to their own historical hourly distribution, focusing on explicit chart view actions.

## Value

Privacy and compliance stewards receive early warning when record browsing resembles snooping, fraud reconnaissance, or compromised credentials rather than predictable clinical workload, sustaining patient trust and audit readiness.

## Implementation

Route immutable EHR audit exports into `index=ehr` with consistent `mrn`, `user_id`, and `action` values. Exclude break-glass and shared generic accounts via lookup. Maintain a ninety-day warmup before applying three-sigma thresholds so averages are meaningful. Coordinate with HIPAA privacy officers before automated discipline triggers.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Legal review aligning alerting with organizational privacy policies; restricted indexes with need-to-know search roles.
• Normalized identifiers: same `user_id` across Epic and Cerner feeds if clinicians rotate systems; hashing MRNs already handled upstream if required.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
 syslog or Database Connect deltas must preserve original event time. Align `action` vocabulary (view, open chart) through `eval` normalization. Maintain an exclusion lookup for sentinel accounts servicing interfaces.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Tune `unique_mrns` floor and multiplier after baseline study:

```spl
index=ehr sourcetype IN ("epic:access_log","cerner:audit") action="view"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(mrn) as unique_mrns count as total_accesses values(department) as depts by user_id user_name _time
| eventstats avg(unique_mrns) as avg_mrns stdev(unique_mrns) as sd_mrns by user_id
| eval threshold=avg_mrns + 3*coalesce(sd_mrns,0)
| where unique_mrns > threshold AND unique_mrns > 20
| sort - unique_mrns
```

Understanding this SPL

**Anomalous Medical Record Number Access Patterns** derives per-user hourly distinct patient counts then compares spikes to each user's own trailing distribution via `eventstats`.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• `bin _time span=1h` defines comparison buckets.
• `stats dc(mrn)... by user_id user_name _time` captures breadth of browsing per interval.
• `eventstats` adds mean and deviation without destroying rows.
• `coalesce(sd_mrns,0)` avoids null math for brand-new hires until variance exists.

Step 3 — Validate
Reconstruct flagged hours inside the vendor audit UI. Interview department managers for patient mix explanations (mass casualty drills, retrospective studies). Tune multiplier from 3σ to departmental baselines stored in lookups when statistics stabilize.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Send high-severity alerts to privacy triage queues with ticket templating—not frontline paging—unless combined with insider-threat corroborations. Quarterly review false positives feed back into exclusions. Consider visualizations: Table with drill-ins, Histogram of unique_mrns compared to baseline, Trend of alert volume versus training milestones.

## SPL

```spl
index=ehr sourcetype IN ("epic:access_log","cerner:audit") action="view"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(mrn) as unique_mrns count as total_accesses values(department) as depts by user_id user_name _time
| eventstats avg(unique_mrns) as avg_mrns stdev(unique_mrns) as sd_mrns by user_id
| eval threshold=avg_mrns + 3*coalesce(sd_mrns,0)
| where unique_mrns > threshold AND unique_mrns > 20
| sort - unique_mrns
```

## Visualization

Table (user, hour, unique_mrns, departments), Scatter plot (hourly unique_mrns versus rolling baseline), Single value alert count per day.

## Known False Positives

Trauma bays, oncology tumor boards, health-information management release-of-information queues, and coding backlogs routinely open dozens of unrelated charts legitimately during a single dense shift hour.
